task:
# Relay stage 2: Research
Task: followup-rename-history-and-newtask-title
Working directory: /tmp/nix-shell.0TxEfP/visual-relay/wt/b355188660a3/plan-20260624121456/followup-rename-history-and-newtask-title

## Task input
# Task rename orphans run history; new-task-with-body shows slug instead of title

Two adjacent correctness gaps surfaced by review of the `show-full-task-name` feature
(commit `42a45f8`, which added a detail-panel task name that is editable, with save
renaming the task's markdown file and subfolder). The feature itself works; these are
follow-ups.

## Problem 1 — renaming a task severs its run history

`RelayTaskWriter.RenameAsync` renames the task's `llm-tasks/<slug>/` subfolder and its
`<slug>.md`, but does **not** move the task's run-history directory `.relay/<slug>/`.
Run artifacts/metrics/status/seals/ledger are keyed by task id under
`.relay/<taskId>/` (see `RelayRunHistory`, which builds paths like
`Path.Combine(rootPath, ".relay", taskId)`). So renaming a task that has already run
severs its history — the detail pane then shows "No run history", and a stale
`.relay/<old-slug>/` directory is left behind.

**Fix:** when renaming, if `.relay/<old-slug>/` exists, move it to `.relay/<new-slug>/`
(in the same operation that renames the `llm-tasks` subfolder, with the same safety
checks — no overwrite of an existing destination). Also migrate the in-memory id-keyed
tracking state in `MainWindowViewModel` so a rename during the session doesn't orphan
live state: `SaveEditAsync` currently migrates only `_boostedTaskIds`. Factor a single
"re-key task id across all id-keyed maps" helper and call it for the maps that can hold
entries for a renamable (non-running) task — at minimum `_liveEventsByTask` and
`_liveTraceEntriesByTask` (others like `_runStartedAt` are for running tasks, which
`CanEditSelectedTask` already blocks from editing; include them for completeness).

## Problem 2 — a new task created *with a body* shows the slug, not the entered title

In `CreateNewTaskAsync` (`MainWindowViewModel.Authoring.cs`), the with-body branch writes
`markdown = NewTaskBody` verbatim — the entered `NewTaskTitle` is used only to derive the
slug and is then discarded. With no `# ` heading in the file, the new detail-panel name
(`ExtractTitleFromMarkdown`) falls back to the slug, so the panel shows e.g.
`my-new-task` instead of "My New Task". The empty-body branch already writes `# {title}\n`
correctly.

**Fix:** prepend the heading to the body branch too, matching `SaveEditAsync`'s format
(`# {title}\n\n{body}`): write `# {NewTaskTitle.Trim()}\n\n{NewTaskBody}`.

## Problem 3 (low, optional) — title-extraction asymmetry

`ExtractTitleFromMarkdown` scans all lines for the first `# ` heading, but
`SplitMarkdownTitle` inspects only the first line. For markdown whose heading isn't on
line 1 (e.g. a leading blank line), select shows the real title while Edit puts the slug
in the title buffer, so saving unchanged triggers a spurious rename and a duplicated
heading. Make both helpers use the same "first `# ` line" strategy.

## How to verify
- Rename a task **that has already run**: its run history (metrics/status/artifacts)
  still resolves after the rename, `.relay/<new-slug>/` exists, and no stale
  `.relay/<old-slug>/` remains. Add a test around `RenameAsync` asserting the `.relay`
  dir moves when present (and is a no-op when absent).
- Create a **new task with a body**: the detail panel "Markdown" tab shows the entered
  title (not the slug). Add/extend a test in the new-task authoring tests.
- Existing rename/title tests stay green.

## Constraints
- VR is general-purpose — no project-specific assumptions; derive everything generically.
- Keep C#/XAML files ≤300 lines (some touched files are already at the limit — relocate a
  helper if needed rather than exceeding it). `./visual-relay check` green; Conventional
  Commit subjects.


## Manifest
(not set yet)

Optionally (3) `SplitMarkdownTitle` and `ExtractTitleFromMarkdown` use different heading-detection strategies, causing a Select/Edit inconsistency.",

In `SaveEditAsync`, add manual re-key calls for `_liveEventsByTask` and `_liveTraceEntriesByTask` (and the others for completeness). (2) In `CreateNewTaskAsync`'s with-body branch, change the write to `$'# {NewTaskTitle.Trim()}\\n\\n{NewTaskBody}'`. (3) Change `SplitMarkdownTitle` to scan all lines for the first `# ` heading, matching `ExtractTitleFromMarkdown`. Low effort, highest blast-radius for the 300-line cap on already-near-limit files.",

(3) If `MainWindowViewModel.cs` approaches 300 lines after the re-key helper, relocate the heading helpers into a small `MarkdownHelpers.cs` static class. (2) Add explicit test coverage: a test asserting `.relay/<old-slug>/` is moved when present and silently skipped when absent, and a new-task-with-body test asserting the written markdown starts with `# <title>`. (3) The shared heading helper uses a single `static` method that returns the first `# ` heading line (or null), consumed by both `ExtractTitleFromMarkdown` and `SplitMarkdownTitle`. Highest structural change but best long-term maintainability and test isolation."

result.answer:
Based on thorough investigation of the codebase, here are my findings:

**Problem 1 — Rename severs run history:**

`RelayTaskWriter.RenameAsync` (lines 187-235 in `RelayTaskWriter.cs`) renames `llm-tasks/<old-slug>/` → `llm-tasks/<new-slug>/` and renames the markdown file, but has no code touching `.relay/<old-slug>/`. The entire run-history system (`RelayRunHistory.cs`, line 14) builds paths as `Path.Combine(rootPath, ".relay", taskId)`, so after rename the detail pane calls `ReadTaskMetric(rootPath, newSlug)` which finds no `.relay/<new-slug>/` directory and returns an empty metric → "No run history".

In `SaveEditAsync` (`Authoring.cs`, lines 64-112), only `_boostedTaskIds` is re-keyed via `MigrateTrackingDictKey` (line 94). The other id-keyed maps in `MainWindowViewModel.cs` (lines 28-44) are untouched: `_liveEventsByTask`, `_liveTraceEntriesByTask`, `_runningTaskIds`, `_runningStageNumbers`, `_runningStageNames`, `_runStartedAt`, `_rewritingTaskIds`, `_rewriteStartedAt`, `_rewriteCts`. Also `_boostedTaskIds` lives in `TurnBudget.cs` (line 9). The `_rewriteUndo` store (keyed by task id) is discarded on rename (line 95-97) rather than migrated.

**Problem 2 — New task with body shows slug:**

In `CreateNewTaskAsync` (`Authoring.cs`, lines 262-291), the conditional at line 277-279:
- Empty body: `$"# {NewTaskTitle.Trim()}\n"` ← correct heading
- With body: `NewTaskBody` verbatim ← **no heading**, so `ExtractTitleFromMarkdown` (which scans for `# `) falls back to the slug

**Problem 3 — Title-extraction asymmetry:**

`ExtractTitleFromMarkdown` (`TaskName.cs`, lines 20-39) scans *all* lines with `while ((line = reader.ReadLine()) is not null)` looking for the first `# `. `SplitMarkdownTitle` (`TaskName.cs`, lines 46-73) inspects *only* `reader.ReadLine()` (the first line). A leading blank line before `# Title` means Select shows the real title but Edit puts the slug in the title buffer.

**300-line constraints:**

| File | Lines |
|---|---|
| `MainWindowViewModel.Authoring.cs` | **300** (exactly at limit) |
| `MainWindowViewModel.cs` | **300** (exactly at limit) |
| `MainWindowViewModel.Helpers.cs` | **299** (at limit) |
| `RelayTaskWriter.cs` | 285 (15 lines of headroom) |
| `MainWindowViewModel.TaskName.cs` | 82 (plenty of room) |
| `MainWindowViewModel.TurnBudget.cs` | 68 (plenty of room) |
| `MainWindowViewModel.LiveState.cs` | 277 (room) |

Since `Authoring.cs`, `MainWindowViewModel.cs`, and `Helpers.cs` are already at/past the 300-line cap, any new code in those files would require pulling helpers out into new partial files (e.g., a new `MainWindowViewModel.MarkdownHelpers.cs` for `ExtractTitleFromMarkdown`/`SplitMarkdownTitle` relocation if they grow).

**Existing test coverage:**

- `RelayTaskWriterTests.Rename.cs` — tests `RenameAsync` directory/file/content operations, but no `.relay/` migration test
- `TaskDetailEditRenameTests.Save.cs` — tests VM-level save/rename including attachment preservation
- `NewTaskAuthoringTests.cs` — tests `CreateNewTask` but only with the empty-body path (title-only); no with-body test that asserts the markdown starts with `# <title>`
- `RelayRunHistoryTests.cs` — tests reading metrics/reports but no rename-integration test

**`MigrateTrackingDictKey`** (`TaskName.cs`, lines 75-81) only handles `HashSet<string>` with `Remove`+`Add`. For dictionaries (`_liveEventsByTask`, `_liveTraceEntriesByTask`, etc.) a similar pattern using `Remove(oldKey, out val)` + `[newKey] = val` would be needed.

**`.relay/` directory convention** — `RelayRunHistory` uses `.relay/<taskId>/` for reports, traces, and status records. `RelayTraceLocator.FindTraceFiles` also uses `.relay/<taskId>/`. The `StageDetail.Refresh` in `StageDetail.cs` reads from `Path.Combine(rootPath, ".relay", task.Id)`.

{
  "findings": "Three correctness gaps in the show-full-task-name feature (commit 42a45f8): (1) RelayTaskWriter.RenameAsync moves llm-tasks/<slug>/ but leaves .relay/<old-slug>/ orphaned, causing 'No run history' after rename; SaveEditAsync re-keys only _boostedTaskIds, missing the other 8 id-keyed maps (_liveEventsByTask, _liveTraceEntriesByTask, _runningTaskIds, _runningStageNumbers, _runningStageNames, _runStartedAt, _rewritingTaskIds, _rewriteStartedAt, _rewriteCts) and the _rewriteUndo store. (2) CreateNewTaskAsync's with-body branch writes NewTaskBody verbatim without prepending '# {title}\\n\\n', so ExtractTitleFromMarkdown falls back to the slug. (3) ExtractTitleFromMarkdown scans all lines for first '# ' heading, but SplitMarkdownTitle inspects only the first line — a leading blank line causes Select/Edit asymmetry. Three files are at the 300-line cap (Authoring.cs, MainWindowViewModel.cs, Helpers.cs), so any additions require relocating helpers to a new partial or new static class.",
  "constraints": [
    "Files must stay ≤300 lines: MainWindowViewModel.Authoring.cs (300), MainWindowViewModel.cs (300), and MainWindowViewModel.Helpers.cs (299) cannot accept new code without relocating existing helpers to a new partial or static file",
    "RenameAsync already has a ValidateSlugForRename collision check guarding llm-tasks/<new-slug>/ — the .relay/ migration must use the same no-overwrite guard (Directory.Exists check before move)",
    "CanEditSelectedTask already blocks editing for running tasks, but for completeness all id-keyed maps should be migrated (even those tied to running state)",
    "MigrateTrackingDictKey only handles HashSet<string> — dictionaries need a separate Remove+re-add pattern with value preservation",
    "RewriteUndoStore is keyed by task id and currently discarded (not migrated) on rename — this is acceptable since edits block during rewrite, but should remain consistent",
    "Existing tests must stay green: RelayTaskWriterTests, TaskDetailEditRenameTests, NewTaskAuthoringTests, RelayRunHistoryTests",
    "New tests needed: (a) RenameAsync moves .relay/ when present, no-op when absent; (b) CreateNewTask with body writes '# {title}\\n\\n{body}'",
    "Conventional Commit subject required; ./visual-relay check green",
    "No project-specific assumptions — VR is general-purpose"
  ]
}
